data.title: OpenAI asks court to dismiss trade-secret lawsuit from Elon Musk's xAI
data.description:
OpenAI (NASDAQ:MSFT) asked a federal judge on Thursday to dismiss a lawsuit that alleged the company lured employees away from xAI, which is run by Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, to steal trade secrets. 
 
OpenAI, in a filing to the district court for the northern district of California, San Francisco Division, on Thursday, called xAI's trade-secret lawsuit an attempt to intimidate OpenAI and "distract from the failures of [Musk's] own competitive AI effort." 

"The truth is that xAI is hemorrhaging talent to other competitors, including OpenAI," OpenAI said. 

Musk's xAI filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in the last week of September, alleging that former xAI employees stole company secrets related to Grok and provided them to OpenAI.
 
The lawsuit marks the latest clash between Elon Musk and OpenAI, which he co-founded with Sam Altman. Musk, who opposes OpenAI’s shift toward a for-profit model, had previously sued over the issue.
 
xAI has separately sued Apple and OpenAI in August, accusing the pair of an “anticompetitive scheme” to thwart artificial intelligence rivals. Apple and OpenAI denied the allegations and asked the court to dismiss that case on Tuesday.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
6:05 AM Fed's Williams Speech

New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams gives keynote before the Klaas Knot Farewell Symposium organized by De Nederlandsche Bank.

8:30 AM Employment Situation 

The most closely watched of all economic indicators, the employment situation is a set of monthly labor market indicators based on two separate reports: the establishment survey and the household survey.  Payrolls expected to be up 50K in September. The jobless rate is expected to held steady at 4.3% in September.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Final

US Services Purchasing Managers' Index is based on monthly questionnaire surveys collected from over 400 U.S. companies, which provide a leading indication of what is happening in the private sector services economy.

10:00 AM ISM Services Index

Producing a monthly composite of general activity tracked in volumes, the Institute for Supply Management surveys several hundred service-providing firms from 16 industries. Slight dip to 51.6 expected from 52.0 in August.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

1:30 PM Fed's Logan Speech

Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan participates in moderated conversation before the 10th Annual Conference of International Economics, hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas, the University of Houston and the Banco de México.

1:40 PM Fed's Jefferson: "U.S. Economic Outlook"

Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson speaks on "U.S. Economic Outlook and the Fed's Monetary Policy Framework" before the Drexel Economic Forum.

data.title: Canada must remove barriers to energy industry investment, Enbridge CEO warns
data.description:
Canada "absolutely" can be an energy superpower, but excessive regulations and lengthy approvals are making the country less competitive, and it will fail to attract the needed capital without changes, Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) CEO Greg Ebel warned Thursday.

Canada has the tools to dominate the global energy landscape, with supplies of uranium, natural gas, oil, and gold, the CEO said - "The only thing that's stopping us is ourselves."

About two-thirds of Enbridge's (NYSE:ENB) C$30 billion investment program goes to the U.S., where opportunities are better, Ebel told the Empire Club of Canada at an event in Toronto, adding that policies started under former Prime Minister Trudeau - including an emissions cap on energy producers, a carbon tax, a tanker ban off northern British Columbia, and a lengthier approval process - have driven capital away.

"The last 10 years have been a competitiveness disaster for Canada," Ebel said. "The next 10 can be a renaissance, if we choose to lead," adding that energy is a core driver for Canada's economy, contributing more than 10% of gross domestic product.

His comments came as Alberta's government said it would back and help develop a proposed oil pipeline to Canada's northwest coast with an industry advisory group that includes Enbridge (ENB), and while the company would "entertain the possibility" of becoming a project proponent, the current regulatory conditions are a barrier, Ebel said.

"You're not going to build a pipeline to nowhere, so if the tanker ban is there, why would you build a pipeline to the west coast?" the CEO said.

data.title: U.S. crude falls to nearly five-month low as market braces for OPEC+ production hike
data.description:
Crude oil futures fell Thursday for the fourth consecutive session on expectations that OPEC+ producers will again approve a production increase when they meet on Sunday, coupled with high U.S. crude inventories, prompting concerns of excessive supply.

OPEC+ reportedly could agree to boost oil output by as much as 500,000 bbl/day in November, triple the increase for October, as Saudi Arabia seeks to reclaim market share.

The latest data from the Energy Information Administration showed crude inventories rose for the first time in three weeks and gasoline stocks jumped by the most since June.

"We believe September marked a turning point, with the oil market now heading towards a sizeable surplus in 4Q25 and into next year," J.P. Morgan said in a note, as potentially higher OPEC+ supply, slowing global refinery crude runs due to maintenance, and a seasonal dip in demand in the months ahead are set to accelerate oil stock builds.

There are also concerns that a prolonged U.S. government shutdown could hurt the economy and depress fuel demand.

But deeper losses were capped by concerns of Russian supply disruptions after news that the Trump administration will provide Ukraine with intelligence support for long-range strikes against Russia's energy infrastructure, including refineries, pipelines and power stations.

Traders said stockpiling demand from China, the world's largest crude oil importer, also limited the downside to oil prices.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery finished -2.1% to $60.48/bbl, its lowest settlement value since May 8, front-month Brent December crude (CO1:COM) ended -1.9% to $64.11/bbl, its lowest close since May 30, and front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for November ended -1% to $3.442/MMBtu, snapping a seven-session winning streak.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

data.title: Trump is said to weigh $10B farm aid plan amid tariff fallout, trade tensions
data.description:
President Trump is considering a new aid package worth between $10 billion and $14 billion to support U.S. farmers hurt by his tariff policies, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the talks. The plan, still under discussion, could use revenue collected from tariffs, with payments potentially starting in the coming months.

Officials say soybean growers, who have been among the hardest hit, would likely receive much of the support. A White House spokeswoman said Trump remains in close contact with Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins about farmers’ needs and intends to use tariff money to help them, though final decisions have not been made.

The president also indicated he would press Chinese leader Xi Jinping to resume buying U.S. soybeans during their upcoming meeting in South Korea. Any breakthrough with China could affect the administration’s decision on aid, the Journal reported, citing U.S. officials.

Farmers face mounting pressure as bumper harvests have created surpluses, driving down crop prices. Costs for equipment and fertilizer continue to climb, and U.S. soybean exports to China have dropped sharply. Only 200 million bushels were shipped this year through August, compared with nearly one billion in the same period last year. Brazil, meanwhile, has seized much of the Chinese market.

Producers like Chris Swanson, who farms in Iowa, say current soybean prices are unsustainable despite decent yields, the Journal reported. Last year’s $10 billion in government aid helped offset losses, and farmers expect any new payments would be spent quickly on deferred purchases.

Lawmakers from farm states have urged the administration to provide support for a range of crops while also opening new export markets. Rollins has been traveling abroad to secure alternative buyers, as China’s long-term investments in South America threaten U.S. market share.

Soybean futures hover around $10 per bushel, but farmers in some northern states are seeing closer to $8. The U.S. has collected a record $149 billion in tariffs so far this year, much of it from duties Trump imposed on China.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Shell-led LNG Canada prepares to start Train 2 even as Train 1 slow to ramp up - Reuters
data.description:
Shell-led (NYSE:SHEL) LNG Canada has begun the process of starting up its second 6.5 million metric tons/year Train 2 liquefied natural gas processing unit in British Columbia, Reuters reported Thursday.

Train 2 is starting up as the company continues to experience technical problems at Train 1, according to the report, just four months after first production following nearly seven years of construction.

LNG Canada is said to be operating at less than half its stated capacity of 2 billion cf/day of gas and exported less in September than in August, with only four cargoes leaving the port for a total export of less than 300,000 metric tons in September compared with 400,000 tons sold in the previous month, according to preliminary ship tracking data from LSEG.

The slow ramp-ureportedly has contributed to daily spot prices that slipped to record lows last week, as the facility had failed so far to drain a gas glut that built in anticipation of increasing demand from the plant.

LNG Canada is Canada's first major LNG export facility and the first on the west coast of North America that provides direct access to Asia, the world's largest LNG market.

data.title: Boeing expands plans to replace striking St. Louis workers
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) said Thursday it is expanding plans to hire replacement workers and boost recruitment efforts at its St. Louis-area plant, where 3,200 members of the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers District 837 have been on strike since August 4.

Dan Gillian, Boeing's (NYSE:BA) top St. Louis executive, told employees in an internal memo that the standoff could continue for weeks or months, and as a result, the company is "immediately expanding the jobs we're recruiting for, and stepping up our recruitment efforts to increase hiring activities through the end of the year, including more job fairs and advertising."

The first group of replacement workers for munitions production and assembly mechanics will begin training on Friday, Gillian said.

Union officials said Gillian's move was mostly a bluff, noting the St. Louis positions require up to several months of training, and obtaining a security clearance needed for many of the defense jobs can take six months.

Missouri's Senator Hawley used a Senate committee hearing Wednesday that was reviewing President Trump's nominations to key labor positions - including Boeing's (BA) chief labor counsel Scott Mayer to the National Labor Relations Board - to criticize the company's stance in labor talks.

Hawley and other senators slammed Boeing (BA) for awarded substantial compensation packages to recent executives while holding out against the union's proposals.

data.title: Range Capital Acquisition Corp. II announces pricing of $200 million initial public offering
data.description:
• Range Capital Acquisition Corp. II announced on Thursday the pricing of its initial public offering of 20,000,000 units, at a price of $10.00 per unit.
  • The units are expected to commence trading on October 3, 2025, on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “RNGTU.”
  • Each unit sold in the offering consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant. Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and warrants are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbols “RNGT” and “RNGTW,” respectively.
  • The underwriters have been granted a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 3,000,000 units at the initial public offering price to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 6, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mirasol Resources announces $3 million private placement
data.description:
• Mirasol Resources (TSXV:MRZ:CA) announced on Thursday it is arranging a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 6,666,667 units at $0.45 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of $3.0 million.
  • Each unit will comprise one common share and one-half of one non-transferable common share purchase warrant, with each whole warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional common share at a price of $0.60 for a period of twelve  months from closing of the offering.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used to expand Mirasol’s successful exploration programs and for general corporate purposes.
  • Finders’ fees may be payable on a portion of the Offering. The shares issued under the Offering will be subject to a four-month hold period, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release
